title: Robert Kraft wants to bring Patriots to Scotland for a football game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots The NFL has nine international games planned for the 2026 season, with more on the way in the coming years.
Scotland fans took in two games in Foxborough during group stage action in the 2026 World Cup.

(Danielle Parhizkaran/Globe Staff) By Conor Ryan June 23, 2026 | 3:00 PM 2 minutes to read The Tartan Army took over Boston for over a week — with an outpouring of bagpipe-blasting, kilt-wearing, traffic-cone-placing revelry standing as a highlight of the ongoing World Cup festivities here.

title: Massachusetts family loses four members in Dominican Republic fire
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The victims were staying at an Airbnb when a fire broke out in the early morning hours of June 13.
GoFundMe organizer Natasha Salas shared this vacation collage of family members as part of her effort to raise funds for the survivors of a fire in the Dominican Republic.

title: One injured in stabbing near downtown Nantucket ferry terminal
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime Nantucket police have reportedly detained a person in connection with the incident.
Adobe Stock By Abby Patkin June 23, 2026 | 2:28 PM 1 minute to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.
One person was rushed to Nantucket Cottage Hospital Tuesday following a stabbing in the island’s busy downtown area, according to police.

author: John Waller
bullets: World Cup Scotland's raucous fan base has moved south, and they haven't slowed down.

title: Fraudsters in FIFA volunteer gear tried to get into World Cup match in Foxborough, police say
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: World Cup State Police said the pair were ultimately turned away from Boston Stadium, aka Gillette.
FIFA volunteers wait before Scotland faces Morocco in their 2026 World Cup Group C match at Boston Stadium in Foxborough on Friday, June 19, 2026.

Finn Gomez for The Boston Globe By Abby Patkin June 23, 2026 | 1:33 PM 1 minute to read Shortly before kickoff in Friday’s World Cup match between Scotland and Morocco, police say two people attempted to sneak into Boston Stadium by donning FIFA volunteer gear.
